name: Texas Data Privacy and Security Act (TDPSA)
summary: The Texas Data Privacy and Security Act provides Texas residents with data privacy rights and imposes obligations on businesses that process personal data. Notably, the TDPSA has no revenue or data volume threshold, applying to any entity conducting business in Texas or producing products or services consumed by Texas residents that processes personal data, excluding small businesses as defined by the U.S. Small Business Administration.
keyRequirements: Right to access, correct, delete, and obtain a portable copy of personal data
Right to opt out of the sale of personal data, targeted advertising, and profiling
Consent required for processing sensitive personal data
Data protection assessments required for high-risk processing activities
Clear and accessible privacy notice requirements
Universal opt-out mechanism recognition required beginning January 1, 2025
Data processing agreements required between controllers and processors
penalties: Enforced by the Texas Attorney General. Civil penalties up to $7,500 per violation. 30-day cure period for alleged violations. No private right of action.
